rulings.Case Title: 75 HOWARD OWNER, LP VS. KBSIII 201 SPEAR STREET, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff And Defendant'S Joint Motion: 1.For Determination Ofgood Faith Settlement (Ccp Section 877.6) And 2.To Confirm Assignment Ofkbs' Claims (Ccp Section 368.5)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 18, 2025 line 12. PLAINTIFF 75 HOWARD OWNER, LP , A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP Amended Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff And Defendant'S Joint Motion: 1.For Determination Ofgood Faith Settlement (Ccp Section 877.6) And 2.To Confirm Assignment Ofkbs' Claims (Ccp Section 368.5) is GRANTED. The revised settlement allocation significantly increases KBS' contribution. This addresses the Court's prior concerns about insufficient settlement funds and accurately reflects KBS' proportionate liability, ensuring the non-settling defendants remain accountable. rulings.Case Title: MISSION BAY 9 LP VS. CHELAN CASSIDY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 18, 2025 line 11. DEFENDANT CHELAN CASSIDY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The Plaintiff concedes in opposition that this action is based on "Defendant's failure to abide by lease provisions limiting unauthorized occupants." Plaintiff also appears to concede that SFRO 37.9(a)(2)(D) applies. (See Opposition 5:4-5 "Here, Plaintiff correctly served its Notice on December 11, 2024, and stated in the Notice that it expired on December 22, 2024, which complies with the SFRO 10-day notice requirement. ") Plaintiff provides no authority for the proposition that notice to Perform Covenant or Quit may properly expire on a Sunday. Here, Sunday, December 22, 2024. Plaintiff points to no language in the SFRO and to no statutory or case authority. The Court notes that CCP 10 and 12 suggest to the contrary and Plaintiff provides no reason, why SFRO, that is silent on this issue, should not be subject to well-established rules and procedures of CCP. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
